description: Webhook abuse — replay of signed webhook events (Stripe, GitHub, Shopify, Twilio), signature bypass, timestamp tolerance attacks, idempotency-key reuse, downgrade from signed to unsigned variants.
craft_guidance: 1. IDENTIFY PROVIDER: Stripe (Stripe-Signature t=...,v1=...), GitHub (X-Hub-Signature-256), Shopify (X-Shopify-Hmac-Sha256), Twilio (X-Twilio-Signature). Each has different verification logic.
2. CAPTURE WEBHOOK: log into target, trigger event (subscribe, place-order, push). Capture incoming webhook in proxy. Note signature, timestamp, payload, idempotency key.
3. REPLAY UNCHANGED: send same webhook again. If processed (state changes again), no replay protection.
4. TIMESTAMP TOLERANCE: most providers have 5-10 min skew. Try replaying within window vs outside. Try negative timestamps, year 2099.
5. PAYLOAD MUTATION: change amount / user_id / sku in payload, keep signature. Server should reject (sig mismatch) — if it accepts, signature isn't validated.
6. SIGNATURE STRIP: send without Stripe-Signature header. Some integrations have a 'test mode' or 'allow unsigned' flag in dev that leaks to prod.
7. ALG CONFUSION: header says sha256, replace with sha1 or md5. Lazy implementations accept any.
8. IDEMPOTENCY KEY ABUSE: same payload, different idempotency-key = processed N times. Useful for refund / coupon races.
9. RACE: send 50 identical webhooks in 1 second. If server processes all (vs idempotently dedupes), state corruption.
10. CROSS-CUSTOMER REPLAY: capture webhook for account A, replay against account B's endpoint. If Stripe-Signature is per-account, won't work; if shared, ATO.

stripe_replay.description: Stripe webhook replay — captured charge.succeeded event replayed to apply credit twice, or modified payload bypasses signature check.

probes.description: Replay captured Stripe webhook unchanged. If server processes again (subscription extended twice, credit applied twice), no replay protection.
Same signature, payload amount multiplied by 10. If accepted, signature validation is broken.

signature_strip.description: Strip the signature header entirely. Lazy implementations skip verification when header is absent.

probes.description: Send webhook payload with NO signature header. If server processes, verification is conditional (broken).

alg_downgrade.description: Downgrade signature alg — sha256 → sha1 / md5 / none. Lazy verification trusts the header alg.

probes.description: Replace X-Hub-Signature-256 with X-Hub-Signature (sha1) computed with empty/known key.

idempotency_reuse.description: Same payload + different Idempotency-Key processed multiple times. Useful in refund/coupon/balance flows.

probes.description: Send 5 webhooks with same payload but different Idempotency-Key headers. Each should result in only one effect; if N effects, idempotency is per-key not per-payload.
